title: Coastal cities rethink life at the waterline
dek: Cape Town is testing a new approach that treats the shore as living infrastructure.
author: Lerato Maseko
body: Along the False Bay coast, teams are replacing a single hard line of defence with dunes, wetlands and carefully redesigned public access.
The aim is not to hold the ocean in place. It is to give water room while protecting homes, transport links and beaches that sustain local livelihoods.
The approach is slower than building a wall, but its advocates argue it is more adaptable and better able to preserve the coast as a shared civic space.
